I just had this genius idea. I actually told Andrea to implement it but he was too fearful and untrained on social skills to even try. But I could pull it off myself! I could go to the local High Schools around here, talk to teachers and as long as they can speak good English I could hire them to do online tutoring! Working full-time as online tutors they could be generating easily 1 to 2K per month. Dollars! Let me do the math real quick…
Alright, let’s say we charge students 24$ per hour. Which it’s fair for professional teachers, international experts! That’s how I’ll market them! Andrea and I will get 40% for ourselves. 15% to him, 15% to me and 10% for the company’s fund, to have cashflow and be able to reinvest. 60% for the teacher.
T x % : 100 = t
24$ x 60% : 100 = t
14,4$ = t
The teachers would be making 14,4$ per hour. That’s 60 RM per hour. That’s a lot for them (I hope, I believe, and on top of that online! With their own hours, they decide, well, kind of, I’ll be a flexible and cool boss).
Alright. Let’s say these teachers in the beginning are afraid of leaving their normal jobs and going full-blown online mode. And they decided to do 3 hours daily. Which is feasible even with a 9 to 5.
3 hours 5 days per week. That’s 210$ per week. In 4 weeks that make up a month that’s… 840$ in a month! Just working 3 fucking hours daily and only 5 days per week!!! Now imagine if they were to actually work 9 hours per day, 5 days per week!!! $2.5K per month!!! That’s millionaire status here in Malaysia! They could have a butler at home even!!! How much is that in RM!? 10K RM!!! Fucking huge!!! They could travel through the South Asian East and even to the Caribbean and South American for the holidays with that kind of money!!! And they would be making that money online!!!
How much do Andrea and I make from one teacher by the way?
Alright! Let’s do the full math!!!
Working only 3 hours per day, at 24$ per hour, 5 days per week… that’s 1440$ per month.
40% of that would be?
576$ per month.
15%?
216$ per month for Andrea and the same for me.
144$ monthly for the fund.
Now multiply that by 3 if that teacher goes full-time with us?
648$ per month for Andrea and the same for me.
And that’s with only one teacher!!! Fuck!!! I’ll hire 10!!!
Imagine just getting 3… one for Math, one for Physics and one for English or programming or something like that. Full-time all of them!!!
$1.9K per month without having to lift a finger after the whole system it’s set up. Fuck!!! That’s pretty good. If I hire 10 I hit $6K per month!!! I got to do this shit…

Bro, I have always been fascinated by my family and my father and I have always been digging about family history because we have a lot of records, old letters, documents and all the good stuff so we had a lot of material to go through on many of my family's branches. Even on my mother's part we have some information (not as much, but still some good stuff).
My great grand father, my mother's grandfather was a German who went to the Caribbean after the war with his wife (she was from Norway, so I have Norwegian blood from my mother's side and from my father at the same time). He literally NEVER spoke about the war or anything like that. He bought a big chunk of land and built a villa and set up a business selling and repairing Harley Davidsons, you know, cool motorcycles. After his death his sons and daughters dug out a metal box he had burry beneath a big tree in the villa. On the box they found a uniform of the SS, a luger and a Hitlerian Youth's knife. He was a captain of the SS. Funny enough his daughter, my grandmother married a Russian soldier trained at the KGB that went on to become a General during the cold war times and fought in Afghanistan during the Soviet occupation and on many black ops in Africa.
That's a truly FUCKING long story. Have you heard about Sæmingr?
Most Hålogaland Jarls descend from him. Like I said that's a very long story but I do love to talk about it, let me know when you're up for it...
See you soon, brother.

With regards to yesterday we'll go over the calls I closed soon to give you some training on closing since you really want to do that and I'll explain you how we will proceed with that. You'll see just how fun it is! I can't wait to show you... just analyzed two main mistakes I did with the first prospect (update and spoiler alert, I didn't collect payment right on that call and he got cold feet before our second call to finish up the process, everything is explained on the post I made with my second account on a community you are not part of right now, so I'll just share the text here):
this was the post:
Broooooooooo, what's going on?!?
Let me tell you the story with details...
So yesterday I had 2 sales' calls scheduled alongside with my 2 coaching calls and with my call with my newly hired appointment setter (yeah, I had to hire someone to handle sooooo many leads coming into my community).
So I jumped on my first call.
Mistakes of the first call?
1-I failed to drive back the conversation to the sale and even the prospect realized and pointed out that I was not cutting him off when he was drifting away (he's really smart, appreciate his inputs tremendously).
2-After he decided to jump on the program and do the down payment we were running out of time because it had taken so long and he had other commitments so I left him off the hook to jump on a second call a couple of hours later to do the payment and sign the contract and I sent him the contract in confidence that he was certain already but as soon as he had time to basically overthink about the decision he simply got cold feet (I feel like I failed him more than anything, at this rhythm I'm not worried about "losing" a client, but more worried about them missing out on the opportunity of their lives with my offer, but that's fine, sometimes we can't help everyone).
What I did extremely well?
-Objection handling with logic and emotional reasurance. It went wonderfully and I increased his level of certainty about the offer to the point that he almost closed.
So I thought I had closed the first one of the day and I had right after that one the second one.
My levels of confidence were through the roof... so on the second call I jumped in knowing perfectly that I had to help my boy with this EXTREMELY painful problem he has been struggling for so long, and I could see the mistake he was about to make and I managed to show it to him without confronting his ego, from a place of genuine care and love. So he saw that clearly and heeled to me and immedietely did the down payment (here I did managed to correct my previous mistake even though I hadn't yet realized that I had lost the previous client yet).
Later the first prospect reached back to me letting me know that he had made the decision to not move forward but was open to it in the future.
Who knows? Maybe we'll sync at some other point and get the amazing experience of working together. I really think this guy is amazing and has such incredible potential that I'd be thrilled to work together on my program, I know just how successful he'd be on it. Anyways... that's pretty much the story...
One client closed and another one that almost closed but gave me a massive boost of confidence and made me pretty happy to see just how much I can help people change radically their lives and just how much interest there's out there for something as valuable as my offer.
Hope this helps you guys and feel free to let me know what you think about it.
Can't really share more details to protect the privacy of my prospects and clients.
PS: Charlie, if you're reading this, brother, I cannot thank you enough. Now I'm also taking a look at your mentor's stuff, probably you'll recognize the hint to it, lol! Man, truly, you have done a GREAT service to this industry and I can't wait to see you on Easy Grow and Nirvana. I'll be there soon... graduation is near!
However you prefer. I trust your instincts.
